Narrative_Meaning: Nines signify fortune, monetary rewards and cash prizes.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Lady card represents luck, freedom and liberty. Symbolic of gambling and games of chance, the lady can seem carefree and easy, but she can turn on you in an instant, especially if you disrespect her. The Lady is the card of random factors, bizarre coincidences, and insightful synchronicities, never dismiss her as dumb luck.

Significator.Plot: The Lady Plot is one of luck. Good luck, bad luck, coincidence and random events. These plots tend to favour certain types of Characters, naive fools, beginners, risk-takers, gamblers and their ilk. What is true though is the Lady often loses interest in her Hooked Characters, and being fickle may decide to start all over again. The Lady can smile or frown upon a side, causing lucky gains or unlucky losses.
Significator.Side: A side marked by the Lady will feel they are lucky, and take more risks. Perhaps they believe now is the time to act, or that they are poised to score the highest in the exams, who knows they might be right. What is true is that the side marked by the Lady will interact with all the other Embodiments during the Plot. They may not be all at once, and might not involve rational conflict, but they will interact directly with Embodiments of all the other sides.
Significator.Character:
The Lady Significator indicates a Character who is a risk-taker, a gambler or gamer.

They Gain Yin when they play a game, or keep a score.

Gain Yang when they make, or take, a bet.

Gain Chi when they win a bet.

They also gain a +1 Success Level on any game of Skill or Chance.
Significator.Scene: The Lady Scene is a scene of extreme luck, coincidence and special circumstances. Yarn-Tellers may play the card to win a game of chance, or bet, arrange some helpful or terrible coincidence, or unleash chaos, as required.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Core. For example: In order to Hook an Enhancer Core, create a situation that only someone with access to that Nimbed could solve.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Hook of this Plot is about lucky breaks, coincidental leads, chance encounters or random factors aligning. The PCs are usually involved in some other business (like a high Stakes Motional Ordeal) when suddenly they run into the next Bad Guy's minions or more coincidentally the return of an old one.
Yarn.Gain: Cash, Car, Companionship (Gain Chi, A Descendant and at least a Cast Extra Ally)
Yarn.Fray_Description: The Fray is a game, it could be a game of chance, skill or athleticism. Games are obviously usually Low Stakes, but higher Stakes games exist. Playing Chess with Death would count as Soul Stakes (at least), a game of Russian Roulette is Extreme Stakes, and so on. Games can be teaching moments, with Mentors teaching tactics in Chess or ethics, morals or any amount of knowledge being passed via the game. Games can be Ordeals or Tests, or can be played out for real if desired.
Yarn.Snag_Description: A freak chain of events, or lack of attention, causes a Character to hurt themselves. Maybe they pull a muscle training, drop a knife on their foot, or step on an upturned nail. Draw 3 cards and apply the most appropriate Card or Trick as an attack on the Hooked Character/Party.

NPC.Description: A Lady is a deceptive manipulating Rake who relies as much on pretending to be something they are not as being a Rake. They often Pretend to be Leaders, Healers or Fanatics.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: Lucky Breaks can be big Gains, Revelations or just a rest depending on how badly Pressed the Characters are. Lucky Breaks can grant all three, but there will be some chance or random factor (often a Liberty or Key Test) to make Gains or Revelations
Yarn.History_Description: The Lady indicates that the history of the current situation is one of coincidence or blind luck. Sometimes this might mean a simple coincidence such as a character presently sharing a name with another character, other times the coincidences are extreme, where they also coincidently lived in the same apartment some time before, and even look similar, then again a situation can often be created by someone winning a lottery, or more likely an illegal poker tournament.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Lady indicates that the current situation is a fluke occurrence, this can be a chance, but relatively common happening, such as bumping into an ex while shopping, to an incredibly lucky stroke of fortune, such as winning the lottery, or surviving a terrible accident.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Lady indicates the solution to the current situation is for the character to take a risk, this may be a wager, gambling game, or a leap of faith.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about your own side, or own self. This might reveal your weaknesses or strengths, if you aren't already painfully aware of them.
Revelations.Info_Description: The Information is about a chance or probability of something, it does not represent a fact, or something that necessarily has or will occur, but rather a probability or chance that something will or has happened. For example the chances of successfully navigating through an asteroid field is approximately three-thousand, seven hundred and twenty to one.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is revealed by some strange or unexpected circumstance or event that lacks a causal relationship. If you run into your friend in a field for no reason, that's a coincidence, if a third friend owns the field and is hosting a birthday party there, not so much.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: Claims are things that people believe the Embodiment has achieved in the past. Often these claims were made by the Embodiment themselves (or their Creator), and may or may not be true.
Revelations.Detail_Description: A Superstition detail can be very subtle or very obvious depending upon how familiar with the superstition the observer is, and how blatant the superstition. Subtle examples might include finding a lemon or potato with iron nails driven through it at a crime scene, indicating someone believed they were being targeted by the evil eye. Blatant examples might include a character stepping into traffic rather than walk under a ladder, or a building not having a thirteenth floor.

Obstacle.Description:
The way is blocked by some random effect or portal, passing through the Obstacle will result in a random effect. Yarn-Tellers should prepare their own table for each one, similar to this "example". e.g.
Dice (d6)Suggested ResultsSecondary Effect
1WoundedAdd 1d6 to Pips/Boon
2Changed Appearance (hair colour, style, skin tone, eye colour)Subtract 1d6 from Pips/Boon
3Changed Body (gender, species, Incarna, Hitch)Swap for another Facet/Proficiency
4Changed Character (Annex, Persona, Core, I-Ching, Facets, Alternates)Alter name / Hitch name / Annex Title / Descendant Name
5Changed Time-line (past, present, future, side-step, retcon) Swap Umbrals for Nimbeds and Vice Versa
6Prize (Chi Gain or Skill Annex Descendant)Roll Twice apply both

Tarot.Normal: Target may leave the Location or Stage (without passing Diff). Cut to destination (where applicable).
Tarot.Crossed: The Target is Intercepted or Ambushed (usually by the Player).

Ordeal.Stage_Description: The Stage is dusty and windswept, there are few features to break up the monotony, perhaps footprints in the dust. The dust could be lying on the ground, a lack of cleaning, or it might hang in the air, a result of bad air filters, or a light, fine sandy desert.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Survive all the way to the end of the slog through dangerous territory, and even try to make it there first, if you can keep the horse alive.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: Gun-Smoke (or smoke from fires, gas grenades, or the like) engulfs the battlefield, cutting visibility drastically for all combatants. This doubles Range and Movement costs (unless Crawling) for both sides and can make hiding easier for both sides (+1 Pips per card).
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
An Encounter with a not necessarily hostile force, often a Group of (Chorus Extra) Monsters.
  Facet    Monster  
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: The Black Horse Paths are actually ancient trade and invasion routes. They are called Warhorse paths by the Hunters, and they may end the path in a Centauroid form if appropriate.

Stress.Description: Stress, especially high stress over time, can have a very deleterious effect upon a Character’s health. Commonly a weakened immune system takes longer to shrug off infections and heal, and the Character may find themselves run down and exhausted from minor illnesses that would not normally affect them so much.
Stress.Rules: Healing times for all Heart Suited Wounds adds the Current Stress as a Chi Extend Duration, even a Distract Wound can take a while to shake off when you are Stressed. Additionally all Hearts Suited Wounds that the Character takes will add Current Stress to their Pips, including the Difficulty to treat and Stabilise (increasing the chances of Wounds Festering).

Trauma.Description: Trauma can be the first sign that a Character’s luck has changed for the worst. Once Lady Luck has abandonned a Character she does not return her favour quickly or easily.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma relieves all current Stress. However the Stress is replaced by a number Unlucky Failure Levels equal to the total Stress Reduced. While the Character has these Unlucky Failure Levels they can Relieve Stress at any time, converting them into more Unlucky Failure Levels, as long as they add 1d6 Unlucky Failure Levels (or as many as they have left if less) to their last, current or next Action. Once they are out of Unlucky Failure Levels, this Trauma effect is over.

Age.Description: An Age of Chance is occurring. During this age unlikely coincidences and examples of extreme luck dominate the society, there is often an air of gaming, gambling, and an association with underclasses, criminality, and  this creates great unbalances and destabilising forces within a society, as the old fabric may be destroyed as some rich become poor, and some poor become rich randomly. These need not be economic riches, cultural mores and fashions can shift randomly, what was unfashionable and ostracizing before can become cool. An Age of Chance can create charmed individuals who always seem to land on their feet, no matter what, as well as cursed individuals who never catch a break.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Chance all members of a society will receive Chance Success Levels and Chance Failure Levels that are randomly assigned, this number is created by Liberty and Wyrd Annexes each rolling a die roll based on Facet Values from all members of the society. For societies greater than 15,000 members assume a 6d20 rolled Score Reduced for both the Successes and Failures, this gives each member a Score between 6 and 120 or between 2 and 18 Success and Failure Levels each. These Success and Failure Levels are activated by the Referee, Yarn-Teller or Author/Player randomly throughout the Age.
